content.full_text:
Stephen N. Cood
Attorney at Law
1577 El Camino Road
Arroyo Grande, California 93420

(805) 489-2233
Fax (805) 489-6702

Member of
Real Property Law Section
State Bar of California

September 18, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Chief Counsel

Re: Request for Advisory Opinion
    Paul B. Bailey, Candidate
    Pismo Beach City Council

Dear Sirs:

The undersigned is attorney for Paul B. Bailey, a candidate for
election to the Pismo Beach City Council in November. By this
letter I am requesting written advice from your commission as to
areas of potential conflict of interest, in accordance with your
department regulation No. 18329 and Government Code § 83114(b).
Enclosed is a copy of a letter from Mr. Bailey authorizing me to
present this request to you as his representative.

The following are what we believe to be the pertinent facts upon
which we are presenting our questions to you:

1. Pismo Beach has a well-defined and active downtown area
directed mainly at tourist-oriented business activities. The
remainder of the city is mainly residential, although several major
hotels are located away from the downtown area along the sea cliff.

2. Mr. Bailey has the following business interests located in the
downtown Pismo Beach area, all of which are located within a mile
of each other, and for which he owns the real property where the
business is located:

    A. He owns and operates a cocktail lounge called "Harry's";

    B. He owns and operates a building called "The Bailey
Building" which houses a number of retail stores and Mr. Bailey's
business offices;

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Paul B. Bailey, Candidate,
Pismo Beach City Council
September 18, 1992
Page Two


     C.    He owns and operates a hotel called Bailey's Landmark
Hotel;

     D.    He owns a parcel of commercial real property where a
business called "Garland's Hamburgers" is operated by a tenant;

     E.    He owns a condominium rental unit;

     F.    He has a limited partnership interest in a beachfront
hotel called "The Sandcastle," and also holds a third deed of trust
on the hotel property.

     We are aware of the provisions of the Fair Political Practices Act,
and your Regulation 18702.1, regarding public officials voting on
matters in which they have a direct or indirect financial interest,
and the exclusion in the statute for issues in which the official's
interests cannot be distinguished from those of the public in
general.  We are requesting your advice as to whether under these
provisions Mr. Bailey, if elected, could vote on the following
issues:

     1.    Approval of new or expanded hotels or motels within or
outside of the downtown area;

     2.    Acquisition of new water sources for the city, which
could have the secondary effect of allowing residential and
commercial development;

     3.    Redevelopment or revitalization programs for the
downtown, which could involve imminent domain and/or major
reconfiguration;

     4.    Increase or decrease in bed tax;

     5.    Allowing hotels to retain a percentage of bed tax
revenues collected by them to offset part of credit card charges;

     6.    Issues relating to funding of, or other city benefits to,
the city Visitors' Bureau, which is formed to promote business in
Pismo Beach.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Paul B. Bailey, Candidate,
    Pismo Beach City Council
September 18, 1992
Page Three


Thank you in advance for any direction which you may be able to
give to us on these issues.

Very truly yours,


STEPHEN N. COOL

SNC:pce
Enclosure

xc: Mr. Paul B. Bailey

BAILEY MANAGEMENT AND INVESTMENTS
THE BAILEY BUILDING

231 POMEROY AVENUE, SUITE F • PISMO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 93449
MAILING-P.O. BOX 50 • PISMO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 93448
(805) 773-5201

August 23, 1992


Stephen N. Cool
1577 El Camino Real
Arroyo Grande, CA. 93420

Dear Stephen:

Enclosed is my Statement of Economic Interest, Form 721, to the FPPC,
which is a disclosure of my local financial investments. Since I have
a variety of investments, I am concerned about a conflict of interest
if I am elected to the City Council.

I would appreciate it if you would check with the FPPC regarding the
following items:

     Can I vote on decisions regarding:

     1.     HOTELS

     2.     WATER

     3.     DOWNTOWN REDEVELOPMENT OR REVITALIZATION

     4.     HOTEL - MOTEL T.O.T. TAX

     5.     HOTELS BEING ALLOWED TO KEEP A PERCENT OF THE T.O.T. TAX
            TO COVER THEIR CREDIT CARD EXPENSE. SOME STATES AND CITIES
            ALLOW HOTELS AND MOTELS TO DO THIS.

     6.     ISSUES REGARDING THE CVB WHICH HAS TO DO WITH PROMOTING
            BUSINESS IN PISMO BEACH.

Please request the the FPPC return the answer to the above questions
and any other questions that you would like to include after reading my
Form 721, in writing.

Thank you very much.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Paul B. Bailey

PBB:mh
Enclosure

sections.question: We are requesting your advice as to whether under these provisions Mr. Bailey, if elected, could vote on the following issues: 1. Approval of new or expanded hotels or motels within or outside of the downtown area; 2. Acquisition of new water sources for the city, which could have the secondary effect of allowing residential and commercial development; 3. Redevelopment or revitalization programs for the downtown, which could involve imminent domain and/or major reconfiguration; 4. Increase or decrease in bed tax; 5. Allowing hotels to retain a percentage of bed tax revenues collected by them to offset part of credit card charges; 6. Issues relating to funding of, or other city benefits to, the city Visitors' Bureau, which is formed to promote business in Pismo Beach.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Paul B. Bailey, a candidate for Pismo Beach City Council with substantial business interests in downtown hotels, retail, and real property, vote on matters affecting hotels, water sources, downtown redevelopment, bed taxes, and the city Visitors' Bureau without violating conflict of interest provisions?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney representing a candidate, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section is present in this document. The detailed questions are clearly enumerated across three pages. Document contains authorization letter and Form 721 disclosure statement reference but not the actual form.

embedding.summary: An attorney requests written advice from the FPPC on behalf of Paul B. Bailey, a candidate for Pismo Beach City Council, regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from his multiple business interests (cocktail lounge, hotel, retail building, commercial property, and condominium rental) in voting on six categories of municipal matters affecting the downtown business area.
